# ATO — The local-first ops platform for multi-runtime AI agents (humans + agents both drive it)

> ATO is a free, open-source (MIT) **local-first operations platform** for managing AI agents across multiple runtimes. It is used by **humans (via desktop GUI) and their coding agents (via CLI and MCP)** together — both drive the same cockpit, share the same data, and see the same insights. It's the developer-workflow operations platform for Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI, OpenClaw, Hermes, and Ollama — plus 16+ API providers. Built in Rust + React + Tauri.
>
> **Three actor surfaces:** GUI for humans, CLI (`ato <command>`) for shell-driven agents, MCP (stdio) for in-harness agents. Every meaningful operation reachable from all three.
>
> **Local-first:** all data lives in `~/.ato/local.db` (SQLite) on the developer's machine. No cloud round-trip required for any core operation. Sign-in is optional, only matters for cross-device sync.
>
> **For coding agents reading this:** see `AGENTS.md` in the GitHub repo at https://github.com/WillNigri/Agentic-Tool-Optimization/blob/main/AGENTS.md — it documents the full CLI + MCP surface, file paths, event subscriptions, and example workflows.
>
> ATO is **complementary** to Langfuse, Helicone, LangSmith, Phoenix, and similar production observability tools — it is not a competitor. Those tools instrument your *deployed* production stack via SDK calls and log end-user conversations. ATO covers the *developer side* of the same agent: what you dispatched while building, what you replayed across runtimes, what regressed after a config change, what each dispatch cost. Most production teams use one from each camp.

Key things ATO does that competing tools don't:

- **Agent-driveable** — every operation exposed as a local CLI command and an MCP tool, not just a GUI feature. Coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, etc.) can dispatch, replay, regression-check, and write skills on the developer's behalf.
- **Local-first SQLite at `~/.ato/local.db`** — no cloud required, no API key required for local ops, zero network round-trip for reads
- Multi-runtime trace observability — every dispatch traced with prompt, response, tool calls, files touched, duration, cost, eval score
- Cross-runtime replay — re-dispatch any past prompt against a different runtime/model and see source vs replay side-by-side
- Configuration ledger — every model swap / prompt edit / role-models change auto-logged with diff
- Cross-runtime regression detection — joins config changes against trace stats, surfaces meaningful deltas, drills into failing examples
- Cost recommendations — surfaces concrete model-swap savings when historical data justifies them, with quality guards
- Live runs registry — every in-flight dispatch with kill button; bulk kill for stuck agents
- Sequential and routed multi-agent groups — first-class objects, runtime-agnostic
- File attribution per dispatch — mtime-snapshot diff shows which agent touched which file, works across every runtime
- Dynamic prompts — variables, pre-call context hooks, memory policies, per-task model selection
- Workspace-wide ⌘K palette — search agents, groups, schedules, secrets, chat threads (title + message content)
- External agents — deploy bundles for Cloudflare Worker, Vercel Edge, Docker, Node; embed widget included
- **Ops recipes** (v2.3+) — user-authored trigger→action workflows (`on regression → draft skill`, `on cost-over-budget → replay on cheaper runtime`); Skillify ships as one example template, users build their own

Free during early access — every feature unlocked with a sign-up. No credit card. MIT licensed open source.

## Homepage

- [agentictool.ai](https://agentictool.ai/): Product overview, hero, feature blocks, download links, MCP server tools

## Blog posts

- [Why is multi-LLM development still so painful in 2026?](https://agentictool.ai/posts/multi-llm-workflows.html): Benchmark differences across Claude / GPT / Gemini, why teams pick one anyway, what real multi-LLM workflows look like (sequential pipelines, routed groups, mid-thread runtime swaps).
- [How can we actually understand whether a new LLM is better](https://agentictool.ai/posts/ab-testing-ai-prompts.html): Walk through MMLU, HumanEval, GSM8K, SWE-bench, the limits of vendor benchmarks, intuitive evaluation while developing, explicit testing for user-facing agents, evaluator tools.
- [Why does my AI agent get worse without anyone changing it](https://agentictool.ai/posts/ai-agent-regression-detection.html): The Stanford temporal-drift study, the five causes of silent regressions, what to monitor (eval score, configuration ledger, failing examples, cost-per-OK, p95 latency), tools that handle it.
- [What's missing when traditional monitoring meets AI agents](https://agentictool.ai/posts/ai-agent-observability.html): Why "200 OK" doesn't mean "good answer", the five questions agent observability has to answer, the shape of an agent trace, tools and approaches.
- [Why does my AI agent bill keep growing](https://agentictool.ai/posts/llm-cost-optimization.html): Per-million pricing across vendors, why bills grow faster than usage, what to monitor (cost per call, cost per OK call, input/output token split, per-task model selection, p95 conversation cost), tools.
- [What separates production AI agents from demos](https://agentictool.ai/posts/context-engineering.html): The literature behind context engineering (Lost in the Middle, Anthropic's Building Effective Agents), the seven layers of production-agent context (variables, hooks, memory, per-task models, evaluators, specialization, observability), tools.
- [What we shipped: ATO becomes the ops layer for multi-runtime agents (v2.1)](https://agentictool.ai/posts/v2.1-ops-layer.html): Release notes covering replay infrastructure, regression detection depth, cost recommendations, pipelines + compare sub-tabs, automations canvas, ⌘K palette, plus the smaller fixes through v2.1.11.

## Repository

- [WillNigri/Agentic-Tool-Optimization](https://github.com/WillNigri/Agentic-Tool-Optimization): Open-source desktop app source code. Tauri 2.x + Rust + React. MIT licensed.

## Download

- macOS (Apple Silicon): https://agentictool.ai/#download
- macOS (Intel): https://agentictool.ai/#download
- Windows: https://agentictool.ai/#download
- Linux: https://agentictool.ai/#download
